activities_projects.title: Faith vs Reality Research Project
activities_projects.objective: Analyze the theme of faith in real-life situations and compare it with Lencho’s faith.
steps: Research one real-life story where faith played an important role
Compare it with Lencho’s faith in a chart
Present similarities and differences in class
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students will critically analyze themes and connect literature to real life.
activities_projects.evaluation: Depth of comparison, clarity of research, and presentation skills

activities_projects.title: Rural Life Case Study
activities_projects.objective: Understand challenges faced by farmers like Lencho.
steps: Collect information about problems faced by farmers (crop failure, loans, climate change)
Relate findings to Lencho’s situation
Prepare a short report with solutions
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students will develop empathy and analytical thinking.
activities_projects.evaluation: Accuracy of information, relevance to story, and suggested solutions

activities_projects.title: Character Psychology Analysis
activities_projects.objective: Examine Lencho’s mindset using psychological reasoning.
steps: Study Lencho’s reactions at different stages
Identify emotions such as hope, anger, trust
Write a detailed analysis explaining why he blamed the post office employees
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students will interpret character behavior deeply.
activities_projects.evaluation: Logical explanation, textual evidence, and clarity

activities_projects.title: Modern Version Short Film Script
activities_projects.objective: Reimagine the story in today’s digital world.
steps: Rewrite the story where Lencho emails or posts online for help
Create a short script with dialogues
Perform or record the scene
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students will apply creativity and adapt literature to modern context.
activities_projects.evaluation: Originality, relevance to theme, and teamwork

activities_projects.title: Ethical Dilemma Discussion
activities_projects.objective: Evaluate moral questions raised in the story.
steps: Discuss whether the postmaster should have revealed the truth
Write arguments supporting both sides
Conclude with your justified opinion
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students will develop ethical reasoning skills.
activities_projects.evaluation: Balanced arguments, clarity of opinion, and logical reasoning

activities_projects.title: Letter to the Editor
steps: Imagine you are aware of Lencho’s situation
Write a letter to a newspaper editor highlighting farmers’ problems
Suggest government measures
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students will enhance formal writing and social awareness.
activities_projects.evaluation: Format accuracy, strong arguments, and relevance

activities_projects.title: Symbolism and Theme Analysis Chart
activities_projects.objective: Identify symbolic elements in the story.
steps: Identify symbols such as rain, hailstones, and the letter
Explain what each symbolizes
Connect them to the theme of faith and irony
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students will interpret literary devices deeply.

activities_projects.title: Creative Alternate Ending with Reflection
activities_projects.objective: Explore consequences if Lencho learned the truth.
steps: Write a 250-word alternate ending
Add a reflection paragraph explaining how the message changes
Share in peer review
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students will analyze theme transformation through creative writing.

activities_projects.title: Comparative Literature Study
activities_projects.objective: Compare themes of faith in another short story or poem.
steps: Select another literary piece related to faith
Compare its message with 'A Letter to God'
Prepare a short comparative essay
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students will strengthen comparative analytical skills.
activities_projects.evaluation: Depth of comparison and textual references

activities_projects.title: Community Kindness Campaign Plan
activities_projects.objective: Translate the theme of kindness into real-life action.
steps: Design a small community help campaign idea (e.g., helping farmers or needy families)
Create a plan outlining purpose, steps, and resources
Present the proposal in class
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students will apply literary themes to social responsibility.
activities_projects.evaluation: Practicality, creativity, and clarity of planning
